reviews.review_msg: I studied mainly using the online interactive textbook. Although his lectures were okay, they lacked depth and often only lightly covered the material at a surface level, which sucked cause the final had a couple of word problems. Do all the CNQ's he assigns, they're life savers for the quizzes, and don't fall behind.

Pretty chill, do all cnq, and READ TEXTBOOK, although the tests aren't bad, he doesn't really explain the concepts very well, therefore reading the chapter before class is great cause his lecture becomes reinforcement.
Very nice man

Not a very good lecturer -- does not explain concepts well and moves too quickly through lectures. However, the midterms were extremely fair and the final was quite easy. I would recommend him overall.
Exams are very different from the practice questions, so make sure you actually understand the concepts and how to apply them.
Utterly shocking. He didn't teach me a single thing. But the final was pretty easy so it wasn't horrible.

he was a nice guy to be honest and i loved talking to him but holy this guy doesnt know how to teach, he reads off fill in the blank slides and does no teaching at all. you have to learn everything by yourself and the sample finals and midterms he provides are not good at all

Decent Prof. Lectures get boring and on Friday when it's quiz day the low GPAs at the back talk over him but he seems to not have control of the class. Accessible outside of class at workshop for help. Midterm1 was fair, midterm2 challenging n not related to practice. Final exam was easier than expected, just put in the work and you'll pass w imin.

My goat 👑
If you're taking this class try to plan going to the Applied Calc Workshop (ACW) after math classes. The ACW is the literal method to do good on the weekly quizzes and midterms/final. TAs and Prof Chen were super helpful for questions in the ACW. I failed math last year but locked in and cooked thanks to Prof Imin Chen. 🙏

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!! Lecture slides are useless fill-in-the-blanks. You're better off teaching yourself the topics or watching videos because his explanations are pointless. The workload is extremely heavy plus quizzes every Friday. One of the hardest classes and a huge amount of people who take this class FAIL and will take it like 2-3 times.

He's not a bad professor, but he isn't a good one either. Lectures are snooze fests and he goes at lightning speed so you're pretty much gonna have to review it while studying for the weekly quizzes. Also speaking of which studying is the name of the game, especially if you didn't do well in hs math; you're going to struggle. Do extra practice too

practice practice practice!! i'm not great with math but it's the only way to learn. focus on cnq's for the quizzes, and don't even consider mock exams being remotely similar to the actual exam. hard class but imin's a nice guy

I took math 157 with her this semester. Her midterms were quite easy but the final was fricking hard. It was at least 5 times more difficult than the midterm. some people even cried after the final cuz it was really hard. For grading, she is stubborn. she enforces you to use the same formula or method in assignments or midterm.
Prof was nice and easy to approach. Course was difficult
She's a good teacher, just seems nervous during lecture because she doesn't have a lot of experience. Very helpful and friendly when you see her at ACW. Overall, she's great and worth taking.

Nicola Mulberry is a fantastic calculus lecturer. She makes topics very clear and easy to understand, and her positive attitude makes the material a lot more approachable. I am very bad at math in general, but Nicola's teaching ability made me understand complex topics a lot better. I would not hesitate to take another course with her. Amazing!

very friendly and knows her stuff. But she is nervous and doesnt seem comfortable speaking in front of a crowd. She goes over simple examples in class and leaves you to try out the harder ones on your own. If you think you understand the material well enough just because you paid attention in class THINK AGAIN - The exams will kill you.

Bad professor. She was all over the place and wasn't very organized. Would have outburst middle of the class but that was mainly due to students talking. However, the tests were not to bad so if you studied properly you should do fine. Final was horrible. I think the average was a fail but unsure since she didnt release the class average for it.

I had a great experience in MATH157 with prof Mishna.  She would come to the ACW and help any students in there that didn't understand the concept taught in class.  She can sometimes be a bit frazzled during lectures or make a couple of errors but she is dyslexic lol (she told us when it happened like 8 times in a lecture).
She is the best teacher known to man. Would take her again!

She makes so many mistakes while teaching and she's not the best at explaining concepts. There was a lecture where 3 separate people asked her the same question and she could not answer it properly. I don't think I learned a thing from her all semester had to teach myself everything. Her midterms are fair but the final was crazy hard.

She is clearly very passionate about Math and this would sometimes mean that she would get into the theory a little more than needed. At first, I was a little nervous about how her exams may be but it turns out her Midterms were easier than expected and completely fair. However, the final was definitely challenging. Overall, nothing to be scared of

Mr. Mahdad is definitely one of the better math professors I have had in my life. I always disliked math but he made it quite tolerable. He is also very passionate about teaching math as well. Do be warned that classes sometimes feel like they are going on for too long and you may find it boring if you can't concentrate for too long.

Good prof. Mahdad takes all questions and will be really patient to answer them through multiple office hours or even in class. However, his lectures, or the course itself are quite challenging. A tip for everyone viewing this review: If you can solve all his homework questions and get the logic behind, you will succeed this course. Good luck!
Really nice Prof. Subject is hard though.

Good professor as he will explain in details is you're not clear with the question but his lecture is quite boring sometimes. I don't have a strong foundation in math so i find this course is hard for me. Should've taken his math100 instead of this. Midterms are harder than final.
Excellent Prof. To bad his hands are tied by Petra.
He would be the best prof if he didnt use Petra Menzs notes and exams. He is funny but needs to make his own exams and notes.
great prof, tests r super easy.

This man is blessing to this earth compared to Steve Gibson. I love how he is compassionate and empathetic. He will give extensions when needed. He does not require participation in class. He explains everything very well and goes over questions nicely. He is a loving man and inspiration to the SFU community. I aspire to be like him. XOXO
great professor, easy tests.

Justin is a SUPER nice guy, but he definitely HATES when people are talking. He's open to student questions and going over any questions not understood. Teaches great and the midterm and final are not too bad. He gives extensions on assignments and is very friendly when asking for help.

HANDS DOWN BEST PROF FOR 157. I absolutely loved having Justin as a prof. I have taken this course 3 times before but always dropped it due to the profs talking so fast or generally sucked at teaching. Justin made sure we thoroughly understood the concepts. Quick email responses, very approachable, goes out of his way to make sure we get help.
He was a great prof at teaching. Does not let anyone talk while he teaches so if you chit chat not the class for you.

I found it difficult to understand him, he has a heavy accent. Lectures are boring and his slides are directly from the textbook publisher. He writes examples on a small white board in a big lecture hall. Examples are also from the textbook. Tests are extremely easy and there is no online homework like other math courses. Easy to self teach.

Ping Ding is an awful prof and has a very thick accent. Be prepared to teach yourself all of the material from the textbook as you will not learn much in the lectures. His midterms though were both fair and the final exam was quite straightforward as long as you did the review.
Be prepared to self teach your self the entire course. He has a thick accent which makrs it difficult to understand him. Midterms were very fair but the final exam was brutal.
He is so nice, even its little bit hard to hear but he always try to give student score
tbh his lecture is kinda boring, but he is an easy grader. Just stick to what he said before the test and you will be fine

His lecture kind of boring and not efficient, you have to prepare and learn by youself alot. But he is caring and give you a lot of paper although he has bad hand writting but i can saw his effort in preparing that, He is very easy grader and exam is the same as how he give you
His teaching was very confusing and i learned all of the material from youtube instead, genuinely nice old guy though and would help you if needed, his grading wasn't harshe aswell.

His class felt like constant torture, unless youa re good in math you won't to great. He explains things on a basic level and then the quizzes and midterm are compeltely different. The only reason I would take this course again is to better my grade since it messed up with my GPA. Don't recomment.
Idk how he's still here

I finished math157 with Nessim Tariq in FIC and the best advice I can give you is to be well prepared in advance if you want to do well in thic course.He is just solving assignments during class and often leaves you confused with so many questions and no explanation.But I found the approach to him. If you want to discover more-jong24094 - email

A very responsible professor is very patient to teach students knowledge, if you promptly ask questions about any points you do not understand, you will get a good grade in the course. Best choice in MATH 157.
Definitely not worth your time.
such a sweetie and his midterms were really easy. dont even have to attend lectures half the time. final was kinda bad but other than that he was fair.
I failed the diagnostic and still got a A- lmao very good teacher.
Fair grading and had good experience taking this course with Paul.

He was a good Prof in general. His lectures were sometimes messy, but his exams aren't difficult at all - very fair and match well with your provided sample exams. He's also very helpful, supportive, and a nice guy overall

This man is the true goat and the greatest PUPPER of all time. Not only were his lectures amazing, but they were also amazing. He gave everyone an A+ for no reason which was pretty sick. He would also always talk about sports with us and do Messi's celebrations when people would ask questions! #TupperedUp #Math #nicee #cute #PaulTupperIsATallPupper

Paul did a great job teaching this difficult class. The exam grades were low so it had to get scaled, but that benefited most people. His Instructor Questions were incredibly difficult though, almost unnecessarily compared to what you get tested on.

Paul did a great job in his first semester teaching this challenging course. Despite the complexity of the material, he explained concepts clearly in lectures and was very responsive via email, helping me with a very pressing issue which I am extremely grateful for. Like any math course, be prepared to work hard and stay on top of the coursework.

Paul is a good teacher who does his best to explain difficult concepts. summer 2024 was his 1st time teaching this course and he did his best. Our averages were fairly low but the class got scaled at the end, which bumped me up a couple letter grades. he's easy to approach and alway willing to answer questions.

I was looking online at how my previous professor was doing, but sad to see that Dr. Gary Sabin has passed away in 2023 July. He was one of the best professor I had the honour to taken a class with. His lectures are clear and you can see he put a lot of work into teaching us, it's unfortunate that he will no longer teach other students. RIP Gary

I was never good at math, but Gary enabled me to score A. He explained general concepts at the beginning of the lecture and then solved every kind of example with all steps. I took Math 157 in FIC, 2022 Fall. I really appreciate him improved my mathematical solving skills and removed all the difficulty through his course.
Rest in peace Gary.

Really patient to help students to achieve to get a good grades. Also, if you have questions may ask him during the office hour to teach. He is very supportive when students are struggling with math. However, the final and midterms are worth 90% of the course, so have to really prepare and work hard on it. Overall, highly recommend him!
He gives you extra points for good process in the quizzes and midterm exams. He tries to make the class more entertaining and is caring. But sometimes its hard to follow some lectures.

Gary is very nice and caring. He is funny and would try to make the class less boring. Homework can be done in just a few hours. Quizzes, Tests, mid-term and Final are not too hard and quite similar to the homework. Pay attention to his review, it most likely to be on the Test.

Mr. Gary is a very respected teacher at the institution and he is very passionate about the class and subject. he gives you instant feedback and explains till you understand. his office hours are worth it. lot of homework . he's big on PRACTICE ! his midterms carry alot of weight. overall he is a great prof.
Best Teacher
Just want to say he is amazing
He is a very dedicated prof. You gotta love him.
Took 157 with him. Though lectures are boring, he makes things easy for you on exams. Kind of shy, but an interactive person. Think math is hard? Take math with him, makes things easy.

German accent overpowers lectures so its sometimes impossible to understand what he's saying, can't imagine how ESL students cope. Very poor job at actually explaining concepts, rather he reads verbatim the notes on the overhead, which you could do at home.
Cool prof. Was nervous at beginning of semester because I think it was his first semester but got over it fast. Clear notes, doesn't try to trick you.
Really nice professor. Review example from notes, his exams are straight from them so very fair.

Failed 157 before, retaking it with him I got an A. Very clear notes with lots of examples. Exams are examples we see in notes, no surprises. Very fair. Light German accent but will probably only be problem if you're very ESL.  Great math prof!
Can't hear what's he saying in class. mumbles all the way. His review session for exams are useless, very bad. No one in class participated when he asked questions. Seriously, don't take his class.
He kept repeatedly making mistakes, after a point I didn't know if he actually knew the material or was just slinging it.
I enjoyed his class a lot. The lectures were from the text book. I would take a course from him again.
he has a very strong accent and i had a very hard time to understand his lecture,so i only study the textbook till the end.

At first, I hated the class. I couldn't understand a word in his first class till later on you get used it. He uses examples and teaches straight from the book which I think is fine. His midterms are way easier than the other prof. So overall he's easy and that's all it matters :)

Harun is hilarious! Really charismatic & approachable. Definitely NOT the worst prof out there, lots of prof don't even care what they teach. He does have a slight accent, and his 9s look like Gs. But just let him know, he'll make the effort to change it. So far it has been a breeze. Great prof! going to miss him a LOT
worst prof ever.

Katrina was very good! She made math an accessible subject by incorporating humour into lectures and making tests simpler than other calculus professors would. My pre-calculus mark wasn't very good, but I felt confident in her class. Go to lectures and weekly math workshops if you struggle like me!

not the best lectures, but made up for it with the midterms and final. All were very free, think easiest questions in the Online questions. Never attended after week 1, just did all the online questions before the midterms and finished over 90%. Don't waste your time with lectures, just do every question and youll do fine. Highly recommend.
Good professor, good slides, and examinations were fair, especially the midterms.

Pretty good prof but her slides were often not very helpful. After going for the first week of class I never went again and only went for the two midterms and finals. Her midterms were so easy. The final however was pretty hard and it was a lot of economic concepts which make sense I guess because it's a business math

Very caring and passionate prof. Very approachable too, don't hesitate to ask her regarding anything. Her exams were very well appropriate especially a for first-year level calculus even when compared to other profs. Finals were a bit more difficult, but overall she may be the best professor for first-year calc you can ever take.

course itself is already hard to i'm judging based on teaching style. she rambles a bit during class but for me personally it just makes lecture feel like she's just a normal person (which is nice, considering other profs i've had). she's really nice, and helpful at the acw.

The professor was nice and always willing to help during office hours. However, her lecture slides are messy and disorganized, to the point where sometimes she doesn't even know what she is talking about. Most of this class is teaching yourself doing onq and iqs.  Exams were very easy as if you do the practice exams.

She is always open to helping everyone, especially during her office hours as she often goes more in depth. I was never good at math in high school, but she made it enjoyable. Exams are very similar to practice ones, and lectures are 1/3 full half way through the term since she uploads her lecture notes. Exams are all very easy, I highly recommend!

Calc 1 isn't an easy course, but she makes it way easier. Lectures are really easy to follow outside of class, and attendance isn't mandatory as you can do all the work online. Her exams are unbelievably easy, which you should do very well if you study. Difficulty varies on whether you're good at HS math or not, but she makes your life 10x easier.

The class was difficult (expected of calculus), but Dr. Honigs did not make it any harder than it needed to be. She gave clear explanations in lectures and provided plenty of resources for success. Her practice midterms and finals prepare you well for the real ones. If you can ever take a class with her, I would highly recommend!
She is a really nice prof, but I will recommend skipping the lectures because her lecture notes were kinda messy… but her midterm and finals are all based on the textbook questions.
dr. honigs is very caring, kind and even during lectures she is entertaining and funny. the tests were quite easy as well and she also provides a lot of formulas. i would definetly take her again :)
shes so funny i love going to her lectures
If only this professor was in the spring section last year because these exams are so easy! I am still mad about Jonathan Jedwab what a waste of time and money.

I did not miss crying over math, but I'm doing so at least once a week. The course itself is quite difficult but the prof is good, explains the content well and provides ample resources for success. The only issue I have is that she did not curve the midterm grades. Maybe that will change for the finals.
Hard course but excellent prof, she's great.
She's so kind and approachable in class. I appreciate the passion she has for math.

-Doesn't really teach, just tells you what's going on
-If you didn't take any hs calculus before it's really difficult

after hearing about this semesters class, i have realized that she was not too bad. exams were fairly easy with some studying, and lots of material was made available to us. lectures were not that great but the exams made up for it.
After hearing about the Jedwab Math 157 section ... yeah, I appreciate Katrina a hell of a lot more. She's not a bad prof at all, I'd recommend her for sure. Fair tests and very nice.

Her lectures are a bit confusing, but she is very organized and gives you lots of study material, practice problems, and helps students at the ACW (applied calculus workshop) after lectures. Grades are given back quickly and solutions are posted when grades are released, which helps a lot for studying for tests.

Her lectures are terrible. Very boring, makes no sense, and she makes the material seem more complicated than it actually is. You're better off learning on your own. That said, the exams she writes are way easier than the homework and IQ's she gives. Considering the exams are what determines your grade, it makes things a lot better. She's alright.

Katrina is a super organized professor. The canvas is very easy to navigate and has lots of useful material. She provides many resources to prepare her students for exams. The exams are fair and all material is covered in class examples. 100% recommended.

Calculus is on the harder side of first year so don't expect it to be easy. Dr. Honigs provides her own filled out lecture notes at the end of lectures which is great for studying. Although her lectures are at times confusing, so is calculus so outside studying is required. Practice exams are provided and matched well with what you get. Study hard!

Dr. Honigs does an awesome job at noting down all lecture material as she posts all pre filled and post filled lecture notes after lectures. Also provides pre-test summary guides for students to study for tests. However, she tends to overfill her lecture notes to fit everything w/ which aren't ideal for first-time calc students

no. awful at teaching and doesn't even finish lectures on time in many cases. acts as if every student already understands the basic concepts of whatever is being taught and the examples gone over in class are hardly ever helpful. you definitely have to learn on your own unless you've already taken calculus before, otherwise nothing makes sense.

Lectures are pointless as she posts the slides online. This is an easy math course and I'm doing fine even though I only took PC 12 in high school. The key thing is, attend the ACW whenever u have questions and watch YT videos for explanations. This is very much a self taught class so just study the chapters and u will be ok. Her test are easy too.

I received an A+, so know I'm not writing this from a place of “I did bad and I'm angry so I'm writing a bad review.” 

Katrina as a person is very kind, but her lectures are very lacking. Her explanations are either too fast or there isn't one, and she doesn't teach with much confidence, causing students to get confused when she messes up.

Worst math prof at SFU. Doesn't know her own material and cannot teach. 80% of students leave her class not knowing anything… Lectures are boring and all theory based. If you didn't take Calc in highschool, don't take this class with Honigs.
She is a nice prof but I wish her exams are like the practice exams :(

It's better to learn from youtube than to attend her lectures honestly. She blows past concepts in seconds and barely takes any time to explain them, and rambles on for half of the class. Most of the time, she doesn't even make sense with what she's explaining and her mic has a very annoying echo that she refuses to address. Would not recommend.

Her classes are boring and the lectures give no sense of direction. Half of the time she is mumbling and when she decides to speak normally, her directions are confusing. The lectures are almost useless, as I have learned the course from youtube.

I ended up straight up not attending class because coming to class confused me further. Learned everything from limits and derivatives onwards from youtube. Everyone did do bad on the first midterm, great on the second, and horrible again on the final.

First midterm was a fail, second midterm had enormous weight placed on MCQ to make up for this. She can be a little condescending in her emails. Made a good effort in class to explain the concepts. Future semesters will probably be better given she's new to the course. Best advice: make sure you study A LOT, that will save you in the end.

She's not a horrible professor, she seems pretty genuine. Didn't learn much from her lectures however. She didn't explain some concepts too well and would ramble on for less important parts. Though I did really like the course syllabus/outline - super clear and detailed. Knew all the due dates and expectations from day 1. Do practice exams!

Her teaching of the course was alright. In terms of her teaching itself, there were some cases where it was difficult the understand the content, and she only ever made a few mistakes. 

The difficulty is set to average as I know a lot of people did poorly on the first exam, which was significantly more difficult than the second exam.

Study.

Dr. Honigs is not a good teacher at all. Her tests are very challenging and she gives little insight as to what might be on them. She refuses to share averages because so many people did bad on the first mid term. She often forgets how to do questions in the lectures, they honestly feel like a waste of time. So much homework. Tests worth alot.

Her lectures are so boring! She doesn't teach well and rambles. She doesn't show average so ppl don't know how ba she actually is. She's not funny and her exams are hard. If she taught properly and then asked tough questions it would be fine but she doesn't. And she is not clear and mumbles all the time. Idek what shes saying 90% of time
nice prof. hard to understand topics but crystal clear
Not a very good prof. Very difficult to understand. Does not teach, merely reads notes. Does not explain anything. Questions are not encouraged and she is not very approachable.
Though I don't have 157, I go to her class almost every time with my friends... Just bored XD. Anyways, I think she's just scary... that's all I can think of about her =.=
Waste of time and money
Terrible prof. First midterm was really hard and the class average was really ugly. The textbook is more effective in teaching you than this professor.

Don't take this course with him unless you have no other choice. First midterm was a shock and class average was a fail. The day before the midterm was NOT used to help students--he merely read off of a pdf file about the last few weeks summarized with practice questions that had answers on the pdf file already! What a waste of class time.

TOTALLY Unreasonable. Gives us a RIDICULOUS test that is barely on any of the things we learned and mostly on things we havent learned yet, expects us to finish an insane midterm in under 50 minutes. Unbelievable that profs are allowed to do this to their students

I never took calculus in highschool, so I was quite afraid of taking this course.  But I was soo lucky to have been in Bruce's lecture.  He was funny, and definitely made calculus enjoyable.  YES, Enjoyable!  It's not the easiest material, but I'd still say it was probably one of the better courses I've taken.  One of the best profs I've had.
Awesome guy!!! Wish he was my dad
The lectures are a hit-or-miss, but if you stay focused and use resources like unstuckstudy ai, you can succeed. Tests are fair, just don't expect much help from the professor.
this teacher literally cant teach, just get out find a new job literally your making the class harder by ur existence dont take him useless

He's a nice guy, but teaching really isn't for him. After 3 lectures I decided to stop attending class to learn it all on my own. He's constantly making mistakes and showing up late to class. Honestly, his lectures made the material more confusing.

His tests are generally fair and based on the homework he assigns, so he's alright in that sense.

He seems like a nice guy, but Teaching really isn't for him. Straight up, I stopped going to lectures after week 3 and learned everything online on my own.

All our assignments were graded 2 weeks before the final. To his credit, he at least posted the solutions (mostly correct)

The worst part is our final exam was cut in half during the exam.
Terrible just terrible shows up late to classes, why are you even a professor, you are very very bad at teaching, very unprofessional. Please resign.
Avoid at all costs. Useless lectures, cut the exam time in half for half of the class and barely adjusted the grades. Makes lots of mistakes in assignments and lectures. Not able to explain.
Don't take his class, he had the worst explanations ever, everyone hated him.
Final was chill just cut the time in half but boosted gr
horrible. the final got cut in half because he wasn't prepared and now we all have to retake the final exam
Cut our final exam time in half because of incompetence
A talented and dedicated prof... hey what's not to LOVE?

Her notes are concise and her lectures are lively. If you do the homework and follow her instructions, the exams will not be that hard. HINT: If you do not know how to do a question make a diagram or just put the formula down. She gives you more then 1/2  the marks for the question if you do this.
he cannot teach. watch professor v on youtube if you want any chance at passing the class. hes also rude when you ask him questions.

Randall Pyke… I don't even know where to start. This man doesn't teach math, he performs it. Every lecture is a masterclass, clear, passionate, and somehow fun. He makes the impossible feel easy. If math were a sport, Pyke's already in the Hall of Fame. Absolute legend.

Class wasn't too bad. Boring professor with confusing lectures but he's free to talk if you have any issues with the material. If you're not strong at math I wouldn't recommend this prof, but also most people were so you can't blame him for your bad grade. 1/3 of the people were even at lectures so it makes sense lots of people did bad

Horrible professor probably the worst ive ever had. Whatever you do, do not take him. Avoid him at all costs. Super hard exams, no curve, and has no clue what hes teaching himself. Learned everything from online chem tutor.

dont believe any good reviews on here he clearly writes them himself. not helpful at all &everytime someone asks questions he answers it so ambiguously, literally even asking him about how the class is graded he wont give a clear answer.doesnt even know what hes teaching like he will literally be figuring out the question as he does it. f u randall

took this class even though he had bad reviews bc i didnt want to go to burnaby 3x a week. WORST MISTAKE OF MY LIFE. literally so many people failing AND ITS NOT CURVED. other 157 classes got calculators/formula sheets on their exams but we didnt. he literally cant teach everyone hated him. hes also rude and enjoys watching people fail. HORRIBLE

Uijt hvz jt b gvdljoh qjfdf pg tiju if fyqfdut tuvefout up gjhvsf ju pvu uifntfmwft boe bmtp jt b gvdljoh qjfdf pg tiju bt b ivnbo cfjoh. If dbo hp gvdl ijntfmg. Xifo tuvefout btl rvftujpot if tbzt "zpv tipvme ibwf lopxo uibu" mjlf xibu uif bduvbm gvdl zpv evnc dvou

Use the caesar cipher 1 letter over to translate this :)

A very bad prof who has no clue what hes doing himself. Gets confused by his own material, and leaves questions unfinished due to him not being able to complete them. Very hard to understand and tests are super difficult. Get this guy fired he doesnt know what hes doing.

Randall Pyke is a pretty smart, eccentric guy. His teaching isn't great but you can sorta tell he tries. If you're taking a course and you already know your stuff like calc 157, then maybe take this class to ride the curve. Everyone else would probably suffer and you can get a high grade. Otherwise, if you can't learn yourself, DO NOT take this cla

Pyke is a good guy overall, but the way he teaches is just really disappointing. His tests and quizzes are based on his textbook questions, and sometimes, they aren't based on anything at all. I don't know where he comes up with his questions but he hasn't taught them at all. Rushes through lecture notes and examples, and doesn't explain anything.

This man has taught 157 so many times it's crazy. Failed the first time I took it (My fault, I didn't study well). So revise the IQ, OQ, and the practice exams well because the same question may come in the midterms or final.

This is an easy course as long as you practice on the textbook questions and exercises on lecture slides. His tests are extremely fair and easy, literally similar questions to what he taught us during lectures. He also goes over what you need to learn during exam reviews. Class is very well organized.

Never, in my 18 years of life, have I experienced such TORTURE. Tamon is an AWFUL professor. He doesn't know how to explain concepts, constantly confused, always stuttering (or saying uhhhh), and complicates everything unnecessarily. Unless you're VERY strong in math, willing to self study and go to open lab often, DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS.

He speaks in a slow and confusing manner with pauses and grunts or other sounds every 3-5 seconds. He frequently made mistakes when doing questions in class and often spent time trying to work out his own mistakes.
Tamon is not the best at teaching but it evens out with his easy tests, as long as u self learn a bit you'll be more than fine. I found his tests super easy.

Extremely unclear, you learn more on your own than in class. Doesnt know how to explain or break down concepts. Over analyzes and complicates unnecessarily. Stutters so much. Unless you go to open lab with thr TAs you will fail. Choose any other prof or dont take the course that semester. He is a nice guy but bad prof

He said "ummm" at least 200 times per lecture, was so unclear with everything he said and could never give straightforward answers to a simple question. you learn more on your own than going to lectures, waste of time.

Honestly Tamons lectures are pretty bad, you pretty much have to self learn everything. After the first midterm i just stopped showing up since i learned everything at home. HOWEVER THERE IS A POSITIVE SIDE OF TAMONS CLASS. His tests are pretty easy which balances out his teaching. I managed to get a B+ and i never took calculus in highschool.

Tamon's a genuinely nice guy, but as a prof… yikes. His lectures are all over the place—he gets answers wrong even with the key right in front of him. The exams are okay, but sitting through class feels like a test of patience, thanks to the constant “uhhhhhhhhh.” If you value your time (and sanity), you're better off taking someone else.

He is not good at teaching. I stopped going to lectures after the second midterm. His midterms and finals were fare. His midterms I would say were on the easier side. His hand writing is hard to understand but in general a good guy.

I stopped attending lectures because I found it easier to go to the TA's and ask them questions and to go over topics. Overall, midterms were good and stuck to relevant questions - some actually easier than the textbook questions. This prof is actually very caring and will take time to hear any concerns about grades or missed assignments.

Tamon is really a nice guy with good intentions. Having no background in calculus will make this class really hard. You'll have to teach a lot to yourself, just attending lectures wont be good enough to understand. For MATH157 its not really about the prof, 50 mins classes 3 times a week means you'll need to put in a lot of effort outside class. e

Honestly speaking, Tamon isn't a bad person, but he really can't teach. I only succeeded in his class because I had Calc 12 experience, you end up teaching yourself everything on YouTube. If you have experience the tests aren't too bad and he will tell you what to expect in lectures and workshops. DON'T take with Tamon unless you are strong at math
SWITCH YOUR CLASS BUT DON'T BE IN HIS CLASS AT ALL. This guy yaps in his own words all you hear is his uhhhhhhhh. Worst prof ever had.
Hand to god, this professor has the WORST handwriting I have ever seen. He says “uhhhhhh” between every word and cannot form a cohesive sentence without interrupting himself.
He's an okay prof. It would be a lot better if he could write a little neater as it's quite difficult to make out what he wrote on the board sometimes. Overall, would recommend taking 157 with him.

His classes were very much useless, you're better off just looking for videos on the topics online. His notes are not helpful either, you won't get any info from him that you don't get from the text, apart from the pop quiz which was not really worth anything, just a few extra marks which don't really help your grade at all.

Lectures were fast, and easy to follow, sometimes difficult to understand him because of his accent, but it is a pretty easy course. Assignments are not difficult, and midterms and final all had marks taken off the total to give everyone a higher percentage! Prof is helpful to the questions you ask him, and will tell you exactly what's on the exams

Jano (Ya-Know) cannot teach for his life. He had to scale both mid-terms significantly and trimmed the total of the final exam from 100 to 75. His inability to translate his knowledge infuriates you as you pay nearly $500 to listen to a person who just arrived from Europe. Lastly, his tests are non calculator and are EXTREMELY hard.

Lets put it this way: when you have to scale both midterms because the class average was a definite fail- we know there is an issue. He tries his best- but he should really stick to what he knows. The material is easy; but he cant teach it.
He seriously needs to work on his penmanship and his english. I find it incomprehensible and I have to rely on my textbook most of the time. Also, he goes through the material in lectures too fast.
